title: Yank a line, paste it twice
summary: Yanked text lives in the unnamed register; p pastes wherever you go.

frames.caption: Cursor on the line we want to copy.
frames.narration: Three lines. Cursor on the second line — the one we'll yank.

frames.caption: After yy — 'bravo' is now in the unnamed register.
frames.narration: yy yanks the entire line (operator y, applied to the current line via the doubled-operator pattern). The screen looks identical because yank doesn't change the buffer, but "" now holds 'bravo\n'.

frames.caption: After p — bravo pasted below.
frames.narration: p pastes from the unnamed register *after* the cursor. Because yy yanked a linewise piece, p drops the whole line below the current one and moves the cursor onto it.

frames.caption: After p again — pasted below again.
frames.narration: The unnamed register still holds 'bravo\n', so p pastes it once more. Yank once, paste anywhere, any number of times.
